summary: Mutation testing results: 82 killed, 14 survived, 0 no coverage. Mutation score: 85.42%.
testQuality: Score of 85.42% is above the 80% threshold, but 14 surviving mutants reveal two categories of gap: (1) dead-code paths in the orphanFeatures block of computeState that have no observable side effects, and (2) missing null-value boundary tests for the != null guards in toProjectEntities.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: ConditionalExpression — changed `epic.frontmatter.meta != null` to `true`. Spreading `{ meta: undefined }` vs omitting the key entirely are behaviourally different (key presence on the object).
suggestions.rationale: The existing epic meta test only verifies the truthy-object case. There is no test asserting that when `meta` is `null` (not `undefined`) the key is absent from the entity.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: set `epic.frontmatter.meta = null`, call `toProjectEntities`, and assert `'meta' in entity === false`. This forces the null-check guard to be evaluated.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: ConditionalExpression — changed `feature.frontmatter.assignee != null` to `true`. When `assignee` is `null`, the guard should suppress the key, but mutating to `true` spreads `{ assignee: null }` without detection.
suggestions.rationale: The current 'omits assignee fields when not set' test uses `undefined`. There is no test for `null`.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: set `feature.frontmatter.assignee = null` and `feature.frontmatter.assignee_type = null`, call `toProjectEntities`, and assert that neither `assignee` nor `assigneeType` appear as keys on the resulting feature entity.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: ConditionalExpression — changed `feature.frontmatter.meta != null` to `true` for the feature meta guard.
suggestions.rationale: Same gap as the epic meta guard: no test for a `null` (vs absent) meta field on a feature.
suggestions.suggestion: Extend the null-assignee test above (or add a separate test) that also sets `feature.frontmatter.meta = null` and asserts `'meta' in featureEntity === false`.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: ConditionalExpression — changed `feature.tests.total > 0` to `true` in computeFeatureProgress, and EqualityOperator — changed `> 0` to `>= 0`.
suggestions.rationale: When tests.total is 0, the division `passing / total` would be 0/0 = NaN. The guard prevents this, but the mutation to `true` survives because tests with zero tests happen to produce the same final rounded result (NaN * weight = 0).
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test that uses `tests.total = 0` but verifies a concrete result that would differ if NaN were used, e.g. `computeFeatureProgress(makeState('development', { total: 0, completed: 0 }, { total: 0, passing: 0 }))` equals `10` (25*0.4 + 0*0.6). Then add a complementary test with `tests.total = 1, passing = 0` to verify the three-way formula is used when total > 0.

suggestions.description: 8 mutants survived on lines 35 and 44-45 of computeState: the orphanFeatures variable and its empty if-block have no observable side effects, so all mutations to that code survive.
suggestions.rationale: The if-body is empty (comment only). Stryker cannot kill mutations on dead code. This is a code quality issue rather than a test gap — the variable and branch serve no purpose.
suggestions.suggestion: Either remove the dead `orphanFeatures` block entirely from `computeState` (validation warnings belong upstream) or extract it into a function that returns the orphan list and add a test for it. Removing the dead code is the simplest fix and will reduce the surviving mutant count by 8.
